frame_analyses.description: Both instruments enter the field and the needle-suture complex is brought into view above the Penrose drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Initial setup is controlled but with a brief adjustment before committing to the bite.

frame_analyses.description: Needle aligned toward the marked target on the drain and driver begins the pass.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle angle acceptable; slightly faster commitment would reduce setup time.

frame_analyses.description: Stitch has traversed the drain; suture is being drawn through while the drain is rotated for exposure.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Atraumatic tissue handling; bite close to intended marks.

frame_analyses.description: First knot throw constructed and cinched down over the slit line.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appears to be a double-wrap surgeon's knot; seats centrally without avulsing the drain.

frame_analyses.description: Second throw formed and tightened over the initial throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Some extra regrasping but throw lies flat and secure.

frame_analyses.description: Suture tails repositioned for the final throw after a prolonged setup period.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Efficiency drops: noticeable pause and reorientation before final knot completion.

frame_analyses.description: Cutting instruments introduced and suture tails managed for sequential transection near the knot.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Cut setup slower than ideal, but control remains steady; knot not disrupted.

frame_analyses.description: Both tails appear cut; final knot remains on the drain with the slit approximated.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final construct intact; no visible avulsion and no obvious knot slippage.

knot_assessments.notes: First throw appears double-wrapped before cinching; right-sided lead best estimate from loop motion, though view partially obscured by crossed instruments.
Second throw appears to reverse loop orientation relative to throw 1, consistent with a hand switch, but extra regrasping is present.
Final throw appears to alternate again and seats the stack securely; least certain hand assessment due to partial monitor occlusion.

technique_summary: V14 completed well within the 600s limit with a secure final knot and adequately approximated drain slit. Needle passage and tissue handling generally controlled, but several pauses and regrasping sequences — especially between the later throws and during cut setup — reduced overall efficiency. Scored at 109.0s / 488.5 FLS with only a small placement-deviation penalty; no major rubric failures (no avulsion, no visible gap, no obvious knot slippage).
improvement_suggestions: Reduce regrasping between the second and third throws by maintaining tension on one tail and planning the next instrument exchange before releasing the previous hold.
Commit to the initial needle angle more decisively to shorten early setup and transition faster from loading into the stitch pass.
Organize both tails for cutting immediately after the final cinch so scissors can divide both ends with less delay.
strengths: Stitch spans the slit appropriately and lands close to both target marks despite limited video resolution.
Knot stack remains centered on the drain with no visible avulsion or obvious knot failure.
Instrument handling is steady and atraumatic, with controlled tightening rather than abrupt pulling.
comparison_notes: Paired with score_claude_V14_001 — see memory/comparisons/V14_teacher_comparison_20260407.json. Δ FLS 1.5 (strong agreement); primary disagreement is drain gap (Claude partial vs GPT-4o complete) and phase boundaries between third_throw / suture_cut.
